plans.name: Plan A
plans.description: Maintain the standard maritime rerouting around Africa, accepting the 14-day transit delay and resulting production halts.
details: Accepts a total production halt on Line Alpha starting November 24 and Line Gamma on November 26.
Avoids Suez-related War Risk Surcharges (WRS) but incurs significantly higher fuel bunker adjustment factors.
Subject to extreme maritime weather risks and container loss potential near the Cape of Good Hope.
Relies on existing carrier capacity without the need for high-cost spot market interventions.

plans.name: Plan B
plans.description: Utilize the China-Europe Railway Express for Shenzhen cargo and Sea-Air transshipment via Dubai for Mumbai cargo.
details: Bypasses the Red Sea via rail corridors for OLED modules, arriving 25 days faster than sea freight.
Utilizes Jebel Ali (UAE) as a transshipment hub for Mumbai shipments, pivoting to air for the final leg to Genoa.
Reduces the projected revenue at risk by approximately 60% by shortening the production gap.
Requires intensive customs coordination at rail terminals in Xi'an and Malaszewicze.

plans.name: Plan C
plans.description: Deploy dedicated charter flights to airlift critical OLED modules and Li-Ion cells directly to European assembly hubs.
details: Immediate dispatch of 25,000 OLED modules to eliminate the 10.25-day production shortfall.
Provides 100% protection for the $26.24M revenue-at-risk for the premium smartphone line.
Bypasses all maritime chokepoints and port congestion at Colombo and Algeciras.
Utilizes premium air-charter partners (e.g., DHL Global Forwarding or DSV) for immediate capacity.
